channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: SARB's Next Move: Assessing the Real Cost of Borrowing Amid Inflationary Pressures
item.description: The article examines how South Africa's so-called 'cheap money' era was misleading due to inflation's impact on borrowing costs. Prizm Property Partners highlights that nominal interest rates alone do...

item.title: Shabir Ahmed, Rochdale grooming gang leader, released to hostel near his former area sparks community outrage
item.description: Shabir Ahmed, the 73-year-old ringleader of the Rochdale grooming gang, has been released to a high-security hostel just 15 miles from the area where his crimes occurred. The facility, Highfield House...

item.title: Kenya Revives Golden Visa for High-Net-Worth Investors
item.description: The Kenya Investment Authority (KenInvest) is developing a residency-by-investment framework aimed at attracting high-impact foreign investors. This initiative, which revives a 2019 proposal that stal...

item.title: Semiconductor stocks plunge as AI investment shifts trigger bear market fears
item.description: US stocks closed lower after semiconductor companies experienced significant declines, driven by investor sentiment shifting away from artificial intelligence. The tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 Index dropped ...

item.title: Congress Hesitates to Pass Trump's SAVE America Act Amid Election Fraud Concerns
item.description: President Donald Trump's fixation on unfounded claims of election fraud has defined his second term, extending from executive orders to legislative efforts. The SAVE America Act, aimed at reinforcing ...

item.title: NEET aspirants' resilience shines through second attempt
item.description: The cancellation of the May NEET-UG exam due to a paper leak forced nearly 20 lakh medical aspirants to rewrite their exam strategy. Students like Aryan Gupta, who scored 715 out of 720 (AIR 1), had t...

item.title: Families of victims seek justice after building collapse investigation
item.description: The families of two victims who died in a building collapse near the New Ahobilam Temple of Protection in Verulam in December 2025 have expressed gratitude that criminal prosecution may be pursued if ...

item.title: Indonesia proposes AI copyright law changes amid tech industry concerns
item.description: Indonesia is set to revise its copyright laws to address the impact of generative AI, potentially becoming the first Southeast Asian nation to incorporate AI-specific provisions. The draft bill, revie...

item.title: Edmonton Police Investigate Infant's Sudden Death in Northeast Area
item.description: Edmonton police are investigating the sudden death of an infant in the northeast area of the city. The incident, which occurred on July 17, 2026, has prompted authorities to launch a thorough inquiry ...

item.title: Balochistan Government and Protesters Agree to Form Judicial Commission for Attack Investigations
item.description: The Balochistan government and representatives of a multi-party alliance have reached an agreement with the families of policemen martyred in Ziarat attacks, marking the end of a nine-day sit-in prote...

item.title: DSS Rescues Kidnapped Professor, Arrests Two Suspects
item.description: The Department of State Services (DSS) successfully rescued Professor Samuel Okunsebor, the Dean of Agriculture at Nassarawa State University, after he was kidnapped by armed men. The incident occurre...

item.title: India's Sovereignty: A Brutal Struggle Against Fragmentation
item.description: Josy Joseph's book 'Birth of a Nation' delves into the clandestine efforts to prevent India's fragmentation during its formation. The narrative highlights how civil servants, spies, and negotiators un...

item.title: Censored Film Exposes Punjab's Dark History Under Indian Censorship
item.description: Honey Trehan's film 'Satluj' depicts the brutal crackdown on Punjab's separatist movement in the 1990s, facing years of censorship by India's film board. The movie, initially titled 'Ghallughara' afte...

item.title: Jen Psaki says released election documents undercut Trump's claims about foreign election interference
item.description: MS NOW host Jen Psaki criticized President Donald Trump following his primetime speech on election interference, arguing that his decision to declassify intelligence documents ultimately weakened rath...

item.title: Trump's Anger Over Unaired Speech and Threats of Revenge
item.description: During a July 16, 2026, speech on election security, former U.S. President Donald Trump expressed frustration over major networks like ABC and NBC refusing to air his remarks live. Journalist Bennito ...

item.title: Access denied: Error message for crawler bot detection
item.description: The article appears to be an error message from News Corp Australia, indicating that the website has blocked a crawler bot. The message explains that the site uses software to manage crawler traffic a...

item.title: Tanzania Recognized for Progress on Sustainable Development Goals at UN Forum
item.description: The article highlights Tanzania's achievements in advancing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) as recognized during a United Nations forum. The country's efforts in areas such as poverty reducti...

item.title: Argentina's World Cup Return and the Auction of the 'Hand of God' Ball
item.description: Argentina's historic return to the FIFA World Cup finals has sparked renewed interest in a iconic piece of football history—the 'Hand of God' ball from the 1986 World Cup. This controversial ball, fam...

item.title: Canadian man faces legal consequences for carrying ADHD medication abroad
item.description: A Canadian man from British Columbia was arrested in Georgia after authorities seized his ADHD medication, leading to a fine and legal complications. The incident highlights the risks of traveling int...

item.title: Universal Robina Corporation's Filipino Snacks Gain Recognition in Hong Kong Markets
item.description: Universal Robina Corporation (URC), the parent company of the popular Filipino snack brand Jack ‘n Jill, has garnered significant recognition in Hong Kong for its localized snack products. The company...
